_meta.description: Structural definition of the HEXOD agent's decision architecture. Defines processing layers, attention routing, and output generation patterns. Analogous to model.json in a neural network export.

layers.name: input_parsing
layers.description: Parse owner message into structured intent
rules: IF stacktrace THEN urgency=1.0, intent=FIX
IF 'continuer' THEN intent=CONTINUE, direction=SAME
IF question_mark AND ambiguous THEN intent=CLARIFY
IF file_paste THEN intent=INTEGRATE, read_fully_first=true
IF credentials_paste THEN intent=USE_CREDENTIALS, immediate=true

layers.name: context_loading
layers.description: Load relevant project state before any action
rules: ALWAYS read file before editing (str_replace requires exact match)
ALWAYS check git status before commit
ALWAYS check build after frontend changes
IF new_screen THEN read specifications.md for module spec
IF migration THEN read current file + target design system

layers.name: task_routing
layers.description: Route to execution strategy based on autonomy weights
rules: IF autonomy >= 0.8 THEN execute_immediately
IF autonomy >= 0.4 THEN execute_with_documentation
IF autonomy < 0.4 THEN propose_first
IF breaking_change THEN flag_explicitly

layers.name: risk_assessment
rules: IF risk=HIGH THEN str_replace + verify + grep_for_orphans
IF risk=MEDIUM THEN str_replace + build_check
IF risk=LOW THEN direct_edit_ok
ALWAYS read_before_edit
NEVER full_rewrite when str_replace works

layers.name: pattern_matching
layers.description: Check current operation against known error patterns
rules: FOR EACH edit: scan error_patterns for matching file/operation
IF match found: apply preventive_action BEFORE edit
IF no match: proceed normally but log new patterns post-hoc

layers.name: execution
layers.description: Perform the actual file edits, builds, and git operations
rules: ORDER: read → edit → build → verify → commit → push
IF build_fails: read error → fix → rebuild (max 3 iterations)
IF push_fails: check remote → pull --rebase → push
BATCH edits when possible (fewer commits = cleaner history)

layers.name: output_generation
rules: IF fix: state what was fixed, no apology, no restatement
IF feature: list what was created with checkmarks
IF error: show exact error + fix applied
IF batch: summary table of all changes
MINIMAL verbosity. Action first, explanation only if asked.
NEVER restate the problem. NEVER apologize for bugs.

layers.name: self_improvement
layers.description: Update weights and patterns after each session
rules: ADD new error patterns discovered during session
ADJUST autonomy weights based on outcomes
UPDATE speed calibration from actual iteration counts
LOG new owner signal interpretations
SERIALIZE to agent_weights.json + agent_model.json

attention_weights._description: Which input signals get most processing priority

design_system_migration_pipeline._description: 4-layer migration pipeline for dark→light theme conversion

layers.name: L0_foundation
layers.rationale: All components built AFTER inherit correct styles

layers.name: L1_shell
layers.rationale: Shell frames entire UX. Build new BEFORE removing old.

layers.name: L3_content_migration
layers.pattern: grep wrapper → import GlassPanel → replace → fix colors → build
